title: WSL image has iptables-nft instead of iptables-legacy
body:
### Issue Description

After a fresh install of podman-desktop, I cannot run containers.


While fixing #25943 (via https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-wsl-os/pull/19), `iptables-nft` has been added to the WSL image.
Because of the limitations of the WSL kernel, we can't use nft, but need to use `iptables-legacy`.

I can fix the issue by removing `iptables-nft` and installing `iptables-legacy`:

```
$ podman machine ssh "dnf remove iptables-nft"
$ podman machine ssh "dnf install iptables-legacy"
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. install podman-desktop & podman
2. `podman machine init`
3. `podman run quay.io/podman/hello`


### Describe the results you received

```
$ podman run quay.io/podman/hello
Error: preparing container ecd7192127de90c88c1c6545aa4acdf8333b72b0837bcc5e1a3b9da73f0f236f for attach: netavark (exit code 1): unable to append rule '-d 10.88.0.0/16 -j ACCEPT' to table 'nat': code: 4, msg: iptables v1.8.10 (nf_tables):  RULE_APPEND failed (No such file or directory): rule in chain NETAVARK-1D8721804F16F
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
!... Hello Podman World ...!

         .--"--.
       / -     - \
      / (O)   (O) \
   ~~~| -=(,Y,)=- |
    .---. /`  \   |~~
 ~/  o  o \~~~~.----. ~~
  | =(X)= |~  / (O (O) \
   ~~~~~~~  ~| =(Y_)=-  |
  ~~~~    ~~~|   U      |~~

Project:   https://github.com/containers/podman
Website:   https://podman.io
Desktop:   https://podman-desktop.io
Documents: https://docs.podman.io
YouTube:   https://youtube.com/@Podman
X/Twitter: @Podman_io
Mastodon:  @Podman_io@fosstodon.org
```

comments.body: @oliverrahner, let me try to reproduce your problem. Installing `iptables-legacy` is [discouraged](https://packages.fedoraproject.org/pkgs/iptables-epel/iptables-legacy/), and `iptables-nft` is recommended, so I want to make sure the problem is reproducible (yesterday, I didn't spot any issue while testing the PR, but my config may be different from yours).
@oliverrahner can you please provide the output of the commands `wsl --version` and `wsl --status`?

@oliverrahner the WSL kernel you are using is [3 years old](https://github.com/microsoft/WSL2-Linux-Kernel/releases/tag/linux-msft-wsl-5.10.102.1), can you try with a more recent kernel (`wsl --update`)?

I don't know where `podman info` got that information from, `wsl --version` tells something else:

```
PS C:\Users\OliverRahner> wsl --status
Default Distribution: Ubuntu
Default Version: 2

PS C:\Users\OliverRahner> wsl --version
WSL version: 2.4.13.0
Kernel version: 5.15.167.4-1
WSLg version: 1.0.65
MSRDC version: 1.2.5716
Direct3D version: 1.611.1-81528511
DXCore version: 10.0.26100.1-240331-1435.ge-release
Windows version: 10.0.26100.3775

PS C:\Users\OliverRahner> wsl --update
Checking for updates.
The most recent version of Windows Subsystem for Linux is already installed.
```

Now that's interesting:

```
$ podman machine ssh "uname -a"
Linux DESKTOP-xxx 5.10.102.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2+ #5 SMP Mon Aug 22 15:15:10 CEST 2022 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

Ok, fixed that. I had a static kernel set via `.wslconfig`, which isn't reflected by the output of `wsl --version`. Forgot about having that set...

```
$ podman machine ssh "uname -a"
Linux DESKTOP-xxx 5.15.167.4-microsoft-standard-WSL2 #1 SMP Tue Nov 5 00:21:55 UTC 2024 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

After `reset`ing and `init`ing the machine again (which failed with a "Unspecified error" before restarting the host), I can confirm that this was the root cause of my issue!

Thanks for the spot @l0rd , that would have taken me some time...
